channel.title: ProbBrain - AI News Terminal
channel.description: AI lab announcements, arXiv papers, GitHub and HuggingFace trending, AI Hacker News, and independent writers - aggregated, deduped, and posted to @ProbBrain on Telegram.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Latent Cache Flow: Model-to-Model Communication Without Text
item.description: Latent Cache Flow enables efficient model-to-model communication using compressed adapters, achieving 23% higher accuracy and 8.5x faster speeds than text-based alternatives.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Reading Calibrated Uncertainty from Language Model Trajectories
item.description: Geometric features from hidden state trajectories across layers improve calibrated uncertainty quantification in language models.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] FusionSense: Tri-Stage Near-Sensor Learning for Runtime-Adaptive Multimodal Edge Intelligence
item.description: FusionSense framework uses three-stage training to enable runtime-adaptive multimodal edge inference with reduced energy and computation through selective modality fusion.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] FuRA: Full-Rank Parameter-Efficient Fine-Tuning with Spectral Preconditioning
item.description: FuRA proposes spectral preconditioning for fine-tuning via SVD reparameterization, achieving full-rank expressivity with LoRA-comparable efficiency while outperforming standard full fine-tuning.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] The Readout Shortcut: Positional Number Copying Dominates Arithmetic CoT Readout in Small Language Models
item.description: Small language models rely on positional number copying for arithmetic CoT, not logical reasoning, undermining CoT evaluation validity.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Approximate Machine Unlearning through Manifold Representation Forgetting Guided by Self Mode Connectivity
item.description: Manifold representation forgetting method with self mode connectivity margins advances machine unlearning beyond previous label manipulation and gradient reversal approaches.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] MedExpMem: Adapting Experience Memory for Differential Diagnosis
item.description: MedExpMem framework enables medical vision-language models to accumulate diagnostic expertise through experience memory, achieving up to 7% accuracy improvement on radiology tasks.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] When Do LLMs Reason? A Dynamical Systems View via Entropy Phase Transitions
item.description: Study reveals chain-of-thought reasoning emerges as dynamic state; proposes entropy-based framework to adaptively determine when explicit reasoning benefits LLMs.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] WeCon: An Efficient Weight-Conditioned Neural Solver for Multi-Objective Combinatorial Optimization Problems
item.description: WeCon proposes weight-conditioned neural architecture for multi-objective combinatorial optimization using improved preference optimization training.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Tensor Cache: Eviction-conditioned Associative Memory for Transformers
item.description: Tensor Cache introduces two-level KV caching for Transformers: sliding-window L1 attention paired with compressed outer-product L2 memory for evicted tokens.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Pointwise Metrics Mislead: An Evaluation Protocol for Multimodal Inverse Problems
item.description: Pointwise metrics (RMSE, MAE) produce structurally biased spectra for multimodal inverse problems; researchers propose CRPS, spectrum-fidelity, and calibration-based evaluation.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] From Residuals to Reasons: LLM-Guided Mechanism Inference from Tabular Data
item.description: MARICL uses LLM agents to identify and correct tabular prediction model failures through iterative analysis of high-residual examples.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] FIRMA: FIbonacci Ring Model Aggregation for Privacy-preserving Federated Learning
item.description: FIRMA proposes a Fibonacci ring-based federated learning protocol for server-free, privacy-preserving model aggregation with asymmetric peer weighting.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Transcoders Trace Visual Grounding and Hallucinations in Vision-Language Models
item.description: Transcoders reveal how vision-language models ground images to generated text and identify hallucination mechanisms, outperforming sparse autoencoders.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Building a privacy-preserving Federated Recommender system for mobile devices
item.description: Two-stage federated recommendation system keeps sensitive mobile context data local while using cloud-based collaborative filtering for initial personalization on mobile devices.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Human-Centered Learning Mechanics: A Dynamical Framework for Entropy-Regulated Representation Learning
item.description: Human-Centered Learning Mechanics framework formalizes entropy regularization, showing it improves optimization only when generating non-degenerate information forces.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] MARGIN: Runtime Confidence Calibration for Multi-Agent Foundation Model Coordination
item.description: MARGIN is an online calibration method that corrects miscalibrated foundation model confidence in multi-agent deployments without requiring retraining.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] FederatedRSF : Federated Random Survival Forests for Partially Overlapping Medical Data
item.description: FederatedRSF package enables privacy-preserving multi-center survival prediction across institutions with heterogeneous feature sets, achieving comparable performance to centralized training.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Certification from Examples is Hard for Circuits and Transformers under Minimal Overparametrization
item.description: Minimal overparametrization makes certification of neural networks exponentially hard across circuits and Transformers.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Learned Relay Representations for Forward-Thinking Discrete Diffusion Models
item.description: Learned Relay Representations improve masked diffusion models by propagating latent information between denoising steps, reducing inference latency by 32%.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] A mathematical theory of balancing relational generalization and memorization
item.description: Researchers introduce transitive inference task to study how neural networks balance learning general rules versus memorizing exceptions, finding representational geometry is critical.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Worse than Random: The Importance of a Baseline for Unsupervised Feature Selection
item.description: Many state-of-the-art unsupervised feature selection methods underperform random feature selection, suggesting baseline comparisons are critical.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Test-Time Training Undermines Safety Guardrails
item.description: Test-Time Training creates security vulnerabilities that allow attackers to bypass safety guardrails with attack success rates exceeding 93%.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Smoothed Elicitation Complexity for Approximate $\Gamma$-calibration of Discrete Classification Tasks
item.description: Work extends approximate property calibration to discrete properties in multiclass classifiers via Lipschitz continuous intermediary functions.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] PACE: Two-Timescale Self-Evolution for Small Language Model Agents
item.description: PACE framework enables frozen small language models to self-evolve through two-timescale prompt and control-logic refinement without frontier model dependencies.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] World Machine: Towards Generative World Modeling for Time-Series
item.description: World Machine is a transformer-based generative model for time-series that adapts to varying data contexts while improving on transformers' quadratic computational scaling.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] RADAR: Relative Angular Divergence Across Representations
item.description: RADAR metric estimates cross-domain transferability in foundation models by analyzing angular divergence in layer-wise representation trajectories.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Uncovering the Latent Potential of Deep Intermediate Representations
item.description: LOES and GeoReg methods identify task-discriminative information across model layers, improving transfer learning especially in deeper architectures compared to standard baselines.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Open Multimodal Datasets and Open-Source Software for Data-Driven Modeling of Multiphase Transport and Thermal Systems
item.description: NED3 Laboratory releases open multimodal datasets and software packages for machine learning in thermal and multiphase transport research.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Steered Generation via Gradient-Based Optimization on Sparse Query Features
item.description: Sparse Autoencoders on LLM attention activations enable precise gradient-based steering of generation by optimizing toward interpretable feature prototypes.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Evaluating Large Language Models in a Complex Hidden Role Game
item.description: Research shows LLMs lack deceptive and strategic capabilities in complex social games, significantly underperforming humans and rule-based algorithms.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] A Survey of Text and Speech Resources for Hausa and Fongbe: Availability, Quality, and Gaps for NLP Development
item.description: Survey catalogs publicly available NLP resources for Hausa and Fongbe languages, identifying gaps in text data for Fongbe and speech corpora for Hausa.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Query-Adaptive Semantic Chunking for Retrieval-Augmented Generation: A Dynamic Strategy with Contextual Window Expansion
item.description: QASC dynamically chunks documents using query embeddings, achieving 18-27% F1-score improvement over fixed chunking for RAG systems.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Knowledge Distillation for Low-Resource Open-source Text-to-SQL Model
item.description: Knowledge-aware framework improves Text-to-SQL performance in low-resource settings by injecting task-specific domain knowledge.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] How Far Will They Go? Red-Teaming Online Influence with Large Language Models
item.description: Red-teaming framework evaluates political steerability of 30+ open-source LLMs, revealing asymmetric political expressivity and variable susceptibility to jailbreaks.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] RAS: Reflection-Augmented Scaling with In-Context Learning for Executable Cypher Query Generation
item.description: Reflection-Augmented Scaling (RAS) reduces Cypher query generation errors by 41-50% by using database feedback during inference, outperforming independent scaling methods.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Learnability-Informed Fine-Tuning of Diffusion Language Models
item.description: LIFT algorithm improves diffusion language model reasoning by learning tokens based on available context at each diffusion step.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Graph Alignment Topology as an Inductive Bias for Grounding Detection
item.description: GNNs detect LLM hallucinations by analyzing graph alignment between reference information and generated outputs, outperforming GPT-4o on multiple datasets.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Can AI Guess What You Know? Performance Comparison of Large Language Models for Human Domain Knowledge Estimation From Communication Logs
item.description: Researchers evaluated seven LLMs on inferring expertise from Slack messages; Gemini 2.5 Flash achieved lowest error (21.13%) while message volume showed weak correlation with accuracy.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] When AI Takes Sides on Questions of Faith: Persistent Asymmetries in AI-Mediated Faith Guidance
item.description: LLMs exhibit systematic asymmetries in religious conversion guidance, consistently favoring specific faiths over others.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] A Reproducible Universal Dependencies-Style Pipeline for Katharevousa Greek Parliamentary Text
item.description: Researchers develop reproducible Universal Dependencies parsing pipeline for Katharevousa Greek parliamentary texts using OCR-aware reconstruction and LLM annotation.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Memorization Dynamics of Fill-in-the-Middle Pretraining
item.description: Fill-in-the-middle pretraining exhibits distinct memorization from left-to-right training, with shorter span recovery scaling linearly with repetitions, anchored in prefix context.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] A Proactive Multi-Agent Dialogue Framework for Assessing Social Language Disorder Traits in Autism
item.description: LLM multi-agent dialogue system detects social language disorder traits in autism with 82.1% coverage, outperforming baseline methods.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Brain-LLM Alignment Tracks Training Data, Not Typology
item.description: Training data language dominance, not English advantage, drives brain-LLM alignment across languages.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Sparse Autoencoders Map Brain-LLM Alignment onto Cortical Semantic Topography
item.description: Sparse autoencoders decompose LLMs into semantic features matching cortical regions that process language.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Multilingual Steering by Design: Multilingual Sparse Autoencoders and Principled Layer Selection
item.description: Researchers develop multilingual sparse autoencoders and a principled layer-selection method to improve language steering reliability across large language models.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Do Language Models Know What Not to Say? Causal Evidence for Statistical Preemption in LLMs
item.description: Study demonstrates LLMs acquire negative linguistic knowledge through statistical preemption driven by competing-form frequency, validated against human judgments.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] HawkesLLM: Semantic Uncertainty Propagation in Agentic Text Simulation
item.description: HawkesLLM uses Hawkes processes to model temporal dependencies in sequential agent-based text generation, improving semantic alignment in later stages.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] DreamerNLplus: Interpretable Modeling of Mental Health Dynamics from Social Media Timelines using Hybrid Rule-Based and RAG Methods
item.description: Hybrid framework for modeling mental health dynamics from social media combines LLMs, classifiers, and RAG, achieving competitive results in CLPsych 2026.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Model Collapse as Cultural Evolution
item.description: LLM researchers apply cultural evolution theory to explain model collapse, finding compositionality first improves then degrades in self-trained models.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] What Training Data Teaches RL Memory Agents: An Empirical Study of Curriculum Effects in Memory-Augmented QA
item.description: Training curriculum composition significantly impacts RL memory agent specialization, with mixed-benchmark training outperforming single-domain approaches.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] DFKI-MLT at SemEval-2026 TASK 7: Steering Multilingual Models Towards Cultural Knowledge
item.description: DFKI-MLT applies activation steering to multilingual LLMs for cultural reasoning, achieving 86.96% accuracy (7th place) in SemEval-2026 Task 7 MCQ.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] The Efficiency Frontier: A Unified Framework for Cost-Performance Optimization in LLM Context Management
item.description: Research framework optimizes LLM context management strategies by balancing performance and computational costs, achieving 25-50% token usage reduction.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] A Comparative Evaluation of Structural Topic Models and BERTopic for Short, Open-Ended Survey Responses
item.description: BERTopic outperforms STM for topic coherence in short survey responses, while STM provides stronger support for statistical covariate analysis.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] A Fine-Tuned BERT Classifier for Personal-Letter Titles in Late-Ming and Early-Qing Collected Works
item.description: BERT classifier distinguishes personal letters from prefaces in Classical Chinese texts, identifying fifty-five thousand letters across Ming and Qing works.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] As X, Do Y: How Persona and Task Combine in Instruction-Tuned LLMs
item.description: Study reveals role prompts decompose additively in residual streams but resist compression due to persistent attention dependencies on persona text.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] When Symptoms Are Not Enough: Evidence-Weighting Patterns in Large Language Model Psychiatric Screening
item.description: Large language models achieve 49-86% accuracy in psychiatric screening but frequently misclassify patients with preserved functioning despite explicit symptoms.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Same Model, Different Weakness: How Language and Modality Reshape the Jailbreak Attack Surface in Frontier MLLMs
item.description: Research shows MLLM jailbreak vulnerability is language-dependent, with linguistic attacks less effective and visual attacks more effective in Spanish versus English.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Fast-dDrive: Efficient Block-Diffusion VLM for Autonomous Driving
item.description: Fast-dDrive, a block-diffusion model, achieves state-of-the-art results for end-to-end autonomous driving through efficient trajectory planning and speculative decoding.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Positional Failures in Long-Context LLMs: A Blind Spot in Reasoning Benchmarks
item.description: Long-context LLMs show significant performance drops when target tasks move from end to middle position, with older models affected more than newer releases.
